summary: ISS-1492 resolved by EXP-244 cluster-wide Chapman citation canonicalization. Part of a 5-issue bundle (ISS-1287/1308/1441/1491/1492) that canonicalizes Chapman 1919 + Yamazaki & Maute 2017 + Kim & Chang 2018 across WIN-010/025/068, corrects the miscited Yamazaki & Maute 2021 DOI (actually Meza et al. 2021 Patagonian Solar Eclipse) in WIN-068, fixes Kim & Chang page range 2040–2054 → 2040–2049, and drops the unverifiable Rastogi 1962 family reference. 19 substring patches; all targets verified unique occurrence. See findings_file for full patch list and per-patch rationale. WIN-068 c12 H4: Cross-WIN Chapman citation inconsistency across WIN-010/025/068. This EXP is precisely the cross-WIN canonicalization the issue asks for.
detail: Cluster-wide Chapman/Kim&Chang/Yamazaki&Maute citation canonicalization. Single coordinated patch across WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068 that (1) replaces the mis-specialized 'Chapman 1933 Terr. Mag. Atmos. Electr.' reference with the correct founding Sq-framework paper 'Chapman 1919 Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218:1–118' (DOI 10.1098/rsta.1919.0001); (2) adds 'Yamazaki & Maute 2017 Space Science Reviews 206:299–405' (DOI 10.1007/s11214-016-0282-z) as canonical modern conductivity treatment; (3) CORRECTS a newly-discovered citation error in WIN-068 where DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327 was labeled Yamazaki & Maute 2021 but actually resolves to Meza et al. 2021 (2020 Patagonian Solar Eclipse, Frontiers in Astronomy and Space Sciences) — different paper entirely; (4) corrects Kim & Chang 2018 page range from 61:2040–2054 to 61:2040–2049 (ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K confirms citation_lastpage=2049); (5) drops the unverifiable 'Rastogi 1962' family reference from WIN-068 in favor of the already-solid Chapman 1919 + Yamazaki & Maute 2017 + Kim & Chang 2018 trio; (6) updates derivative age-arithmetic in WIN-025 (93-year / 90-year references → 107-year / over-a-century). 19 substring patches (6 WIN-010, 7 WIN-025, 6 WIN-068). All 'before' strings verified unique occurrence (count=1) on 2026-04-24. One dry-apply run confirmed all patches apply cleanly with no residual Chapman 1933 / Yamazaki 2021 / Kim & Chang 2054 / Rastogi references cluster-wide. Closes ISS-1287, ISS-1308, ISS-1441, ISS-1491, ISS-1492 together.

cross_win_consistency_note: Post-patch cluster state: all three WINs cite the same canonical trio (Chapman 1919; Yamazaki & Maute 2017; Kim & Chang 2018). No residual Chapman 1933 references in the Sq-mechanism context. No residual 'Yamazaki & Maute 2021 DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327' miscitations. Kim & Chang page range consistent at 2040–2049 cluster-wide. Rastogi 1962 dropped (was only in WIN-068; unverifiable as eclipse-specific — see EXP anticipated_objections).
newly_discovered_citation_errors: Yamazaki & Maute (2021) DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327 in WIN-068 finding/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text — DOI actually belongs to Meza et al. 2021 (Patagonian Solar Eclipse). This was VERIFIED as correct by the curmudgeon c13 citation_check — the c13 check must have only validated HTTP resolution, not author/year match. This EXP corrects it.

Kim & Chang (2018) page range 61:2040–2054 — real lastpage is 2049 per ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K. 5-page overstatement in a 10-page paper. Fixed in WIN-010/025/068 detail_verdict_text and WIN-068 detail_evidence.
do_not: Do NOT apply the 19 patches piecemeal across multiple decider runs — they are a coordinated cluster-canonicalization; applying some but not all reintroduces the intra-WIN / cross-WIN inconsistency that created the bug in the first place.

Do NOT 'fix' the Rastogi 1962 drop by re-adding it — we cannot verify a specific eclipse-Sq Rastogi 1962 paper, and an unverifiable citation is worse than no citation. If a specific paper is later located, a future EXP can add it with full venue/DOI.
Do NOT propagate the Yamazaki & Maute 2017 citation to WINs outside the eclipse cluster without separate verification — this EXP's scope is strictly WIN-010/025/068.
Do NOT mark individual bundled issues resolved without confirming all 19 patches applied — every issue in the bundle requires the full canonicalization to be truly resolved.
